etymology.description: 艹(艹) (cǎo, "grass; plant") + 疏(疏) (shū, "疏") = plant meaning + phonetic value -> vegetables
etymology.interpretation: The character 蔬(蔬) (shū, "vegetables") combines 艹(艹) (cǎo, "grass; plant") to indicate a plant-related concept with 疏(疏) (shū) to provide the phonetic value, reflecting its meaning related to garden produce.

examples.chinese: 妈妈挑选新鲜蔬(媽媽挑選新鮮蔬)，为家人准备美味料理(為家人準備美味料理)。
examples.pinyin: Māmā tiāo xuǎn xīnxiān shū, wèi jiārén zhǔnbèi měiwèi liàolǐ。
examples.english: Mom selects fresh vegetables to prepare delicious meals for the family.

examples.chinese: 农田中的蔬生长得茂盛(農田中的蔬生長得茂盛)。
examples.pinyin: Nóngtián zhōng de shū shēngzhǎng dé màoshèng。
examples.english: The vegetables in the farm grow vigorously.

examples.chinese: 月下清露滋养蔬(蔬)，诗情画意漫溢庭院(詩情畫意漫溢庭院)。
examples.pinyin: Yuèxià qīnglù zīyǎng shū, shīqíng huàyì màn yì tíngyuàn。
examples.english: Under the moonlight, dewdrops nurture the greens while poetic charm fills the courtyard.
